aliases: supersede a progress message by key
telegram lark and webhook push notifications
what events can reach my inbox
blurb: Changing how inbox messages are produced, stored, or shown — send_to_user progress messages, the declared notification event bus (proposals / drift / grades / permission / plan), push fan-out, or the Inbox UI.
intent: How an inbox message reaches the human. Two producers feed one store: (1) agent-authored `send_to_user` MCP calls — the session-blind stdio server only declares the schema, and the PostToolUse hook (which alone knows the session) persists them through the single `store.record_message` writer; (2) a declared notification event bus (`events.py`) over which every non-`send_to_user` system event — an agent blocked on a permission/plan, an external proposal drafted, wiki content drift, a session grade landing, a topic proposed for suppression — is declared once in `REGISTRY` and published through one `emit`, routing to the same writer with a deep-link URL. High-severity types fan out to three pluggable push channels (webhook / Telegram / Lark-Feishu); the Flask API + Vue Inbox render the per-session feed and cross-session unread badge; `regin events list` / `GET /api/events/kinds` enumerate the bus.
label: Inbox messages & the notification event bus (send_to_user inbox message store, cross-session Inbox, declared event design, push fan-out)
